topic: StarCraft Brood War strategy commentary -- live match, Flash vs Ample
domain: StarCraft Brood War (original 1998 RTS) -- professional match commentary
scene: Live professional BW match between two Terran players, Flash and Ample, being analyzed by two commentators. Both players opened into a double-factory build for vulture-based map control -- the standard TvT path. Ample made an unusual early barracks move that baffled the analysts, then pulled a unit back to deny Flash's scouting. The big reveal: Ample diverged from the expected third-factory path and is instead going Starport. Flash is committing to vultures to press ground control before Ample's air units can arrive.

entities.name: Flash
entities.one_line: Flash is one of the most celebrated BW pros ever, widely regarded as the greatest of all time -- known for clean macro and mechanics, not cheese or gimmicks.
entities.source_quote: Flash would never cheese Ample on this map

entities.name: Ample
entities.one_line: Ample opened with a confusing barracks placement and is now going Starport instead of the standard third-factory follow-up -- the defining strategic gamble of this game.
entities.source_quote: Flash would never cheese Ample on this map

entities.name: Clash
entities.one_line: Another player name dropped once as a comparison -- someone the analyst thought might plausibly run Ample's unusual barracks rush.
entities.source_quote: I could have seen Clash doing it because it's like maybe Amp was desperate

glossary.definition: Terran main structure; taking an early second Command Center signals an economic rather than aggressive opening
glossary.source_quote: Flash just has a command center here

glossary.definition: Terran infantry production building; Ample placing one early on was seen as bizarre and aggressive, suggesting a rush attempt
glossary.source_quote: it's such a weird thing to open up with a barracks here

glossary.definition: Terran building that produces mechanical units -- Vultures, Siege Tanks, Goliaths; both players are going double-factory here
glossary.source_quote: three factory vulture oh nice block with the sd

glossary.definition: Build order where Terran builds two Factories simultaneously; standard TvT opener for establishing vulture-based map control
glossary.source_quote: So both sides are going for double-factor, right?

glossary.definition: Fast Terran mechanical unit from the Factory; core unit for map control and harassment in TvT; can lay Spider Mines
glossary.source_quote: three factory vulture oh nice block with the sd

glossary.definition: Terran air-unit production building; Ample going Starport instead of a third factory is the major strategic split this game -- commentators say it is well-suited to this map
glossary.source_quote: he's doing Starport. There you go. Starport is so good on this map

glossary.definition: Terran heavy artillery unit from the Factory; can switch to immobile Siege mode for high-damage area denial; standard in the double-factory follow-up
Terran mech unit effective against both ground and air targets; part of standard double-factory follow-up in TvT
An unconventional, high-risk all-in strategy; analysts note Flash would never attempt this on this particular map
glossary.source_quote: Flash would never cheese Ample on this map

glossary.definition: Terran supply-providing structure; placed here to wall off a path and block an enemy unit from entering the base

narratives.title: Reaper opener decision tree
narratives.summary: Early discussion now superseded by confirmed game context: this is TvT, double-factory opening. The prior 'Reaper' framing was almost certainly a Whisper artifact.

narratives.title: Ample's confusing early barracks
narratives.summary: Ample opened with a barracks in an unusual position, which analysts called bizarre. Their current read: Ample used it as a mind-game, pulling a unit back to deny Flash's scouting and force a misread.

narratives.title: Ample's Starport divergence vs Flash's vulture push
narratives.summary: Ample is going Starport where the standard path would be a third factory; analysts view Starport as strong on this map. Flash is committing to vultures -- if he dominates the ground before Ample's air arrives, the gamble may backfire on Ample.

key_moments.what: Flash and Ample named as the two players; Flash described as unlikely to cheese on this map
key_moments.why_it_matters: Establishes meta expectation: Flash plays clean textbook macro, not gimmicks -- so his movements carry real strategic intent

key_moments.what: Flash apparently sent a unit (likely an SCV) on a wide scouting loop instead of pressing forward
key_moments.why_it_matters: Unusual routing; analysts think it was purely for map information, not aggression -- suggests Flash lacked a clean read on Ample's build

key_moments.what: Flash has a Command Center already established
key_moments.why_it_matters: Fast CC signals Flash is playing an economic, not all-in, game -- he is not panicking despite the unusual opener from Ample

key_moments.what: Ample pulls his unit back to block Flash from scouting inside his base
key_moments.why_it_matters: The early barracks deception partially worked: Ample is actively denying information, buying time to hide his actual build path

key_moments.what: Analysts call the early barracks bizarre; only Clash or a desperate player would normally do it
key_moments.why_it_matters: Ample was playing off-script, likely trying to create an unreadable opening that forces Flash to guess

key_moments.what: Supply Depot used to block an enemy unit from entering
key_moments.why_it_matters: Terran walling with Supply Depots is a common defensive technique; denying entry buys time for base development
Sets up the vulture race; whoever wins map control with vultures often controls the pace of the mid-game

key_moments.what: Flash has slightly more SCVs (workers) and is a step ahead in development
key_moments.why_it_matters: Small early worker leads compound over time in macro games -- Flash may have a subtle economic edge

key_moments.what: Ample goes Starport instead of third factory
key_moments.why_it_matters: Major strategic split: air units vs pure ground mech; commentators believe Starport is well-suited to this map's layout

key_moments.what: Flash committing to vultures to contest map control while Ample's Starport builds
key_moments.why_it_matters: If Flash's vultures dominate the ground before Ample's air units are ready, Ample's gamble becomes very costly

key_moments.what: A scouting unit missed six consecutive shots trying to gain vision
key_moments.why_it_matters: Lost scouting opportunity; Flash may be heading into the mid-game with incomplete information on Ample's exact tech

key_moments.what: Three of Ample's vultures moving around the map
key_moments.why_it_matters: Ample is maintaining vulture pressure even while investing in Starport -- contesting on two fronts simultaneously

do_not_say.reason: Reaper unit does not exist in BW; likely Whisper mishearing of Reaver or another unit
This is Brood War, not SC2; different units, different meta
